description: Renovate configuration for homelab Helm charts with automated chart version bumping
dependencyDashboardHeader:
Tracks dependency updates for the homelab Helm charts.

**After merging**: CI publishes updated charts to the [OCI registry](https://registry.example.com/example-org/helm-charts); [argo-apps](https://source.example.com/example-org/argo-apps) picks them up on its next Renovate run.

---

packageRules.description: Chart app updates - graduated stabilization soak by update type (patch 1d / minor 3d / major 7d). NOTE: config:best-practices (via config:recommended) sets minimumReleaseAgeBehaviour=timestamp-required, so a release WITHOUT a release timestamp is held 'pending' indefinitely. All current chart images live on ghcr.io / quay.io / docker.io, which return timestamps, so none stall. If a timestamp-less registry (e.g. lscr.io) is ever added, set minimumReleaseAgeBehaviour=timestamp-or-age for those packages so they don't hang. Security/CVE updates are exempt via vulnerabilityAlerts. Digest-only re-pins carry no soak (handled by the digest rule below).
Docker digest pinning - Pin floating tags to reproducible digests

CI Docker base image - Group with CI pipeline Docker versions for atomic updates. pinDigests:false (not manager-scoped): the docker base ARG carries tag@sha256 and is seen by both the native dockerfile manager and the custom.regex annotation; with pinDigests:true the inherited docker:pinDigests probes a malformed `manifests/<tag>@sha256:<digest>` HEAD that 404s ('Could not determine new digest'). false suppresses the probe; a real tag bump still rewrites tag+digest via the autoReplaceStringTemplate, so the digest stays current.
Allow unstable versions for SearXNG (rolling release tags)
Allow date-based versions for IT-Tools (date+commit tag format)

pgAdmin4 - restrict to semver tags. Docker Hub also publishes date-formatted tags (2021-12-15-2 etc). allowedVersions does ALL the filtering — only semver tags become PRs. maxMajorIncrement:0 means 'no cap on the major-version jump' (0 = unlimited per Renovate docs), so a large but legitimate semver major bump is never withheld; the date-tag noise is already excluded by allowedVersions.

FreshRSS - restrict to semver tags. Docker Hub also publishes integer-only tags (7974, 8392…). allowedVersions excludes those; maxMajorIncrement:0 (unlimited major jump) keeps Renovate from withholding a large-but-legitimate semver major bump. Same rationale as pgadmin4 above.

LiteLLM database - restrict to release tags, excluding prereleases and junk. BerriAI publishes one-off tags (1819_stable_patch-…) which Renovate would otherwise read as massive major bumps, plus -rc.N / -dev.N prereleases. allowedVersions admits plain vX.Y.Z and the optional -stable / -stable.patch.N suffixes, and nothing else; maxMajorIncrement:0 (unlimited major jump) then ensures a legitimate bump is never withheld by an increment cap. NOTE: this pattern previously required the -stable.patch.N suffix, which BerriAI stopped publishing after v1.83.14 — that silently pinned the chart at 1.83 for months while upstream shipped through 1.92. Keep this permissive enough to survive upstream renaming their tags again.
Chart image VERSION updates (tag bumps) - auto-bumps chart patch version
Chart image DIGEST-only re-pins - separate branch so they never collide with tag bumps for the same image

prFooter:
This MR was created automatically by Renovate. Review changes carefully before merging.

**After merging**: The CI/CD pipeline will publish updated charts to the [OCI registry](https://registry.example.com/example-org/helm-charts).
